title: Long-Term and Short-Term Memory Architecture
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Token Efficiency
description: Three-column architecture diagram showing how short-term turns flow through compaction into long-term memory files, with per-turn cost.

learningObjectives: Explain how short-term and long-term memory differ in lifecycle, retrieval, and per-turn token cost.
prerequisites: Chapter 16: context window, system prompt, turn-by-turn cost

keyQuestions: What is the lifecycle difference between short-term and long-term memory?
Why does per-turn cost drop after compaction?
When should an operator manually compact?
commonMisconceptions: Long-term memory is included in every turn
Compaction is the same as forgetting
